author: ngchenghow
description: This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff. Organize notes into projects. Each project tracks its folders, notes and last-open tabs - click a project to instantly restore its workspace.

manifest.name: Project View

name: Project View

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new in 1.0.3

### Fix: "Link to Google Drive folder" no longer disappears before sign-in

The **Link to Google Drive folder** option was only shown after completing Google OAuth sign-in. Since linking only stores a folder ID and a local-folder mapping (no files are transferred), it's now available as soon as Google Drive credentials are configured — you can link a folder first and sign in later before your first upload/download.

- **Link to Google Drive folder** now requires only that Drive credentials are configured.
- **Upload to Google Drive as new folder** still requires an active connection, since it transfers files immediately.
- Download / Upload / Unlink behaviour for already-linked projects is unchanged.

### Internal: tighter vault enumeration

- The folder picker now reads Obsidian's in-memory index instead of walking the whole vault tree.
- The note picker excludes files already in the project, so it only lists genuinely addable notes.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new in 1.0.2

### Google Drive — link, unlink, and upload as new folder

Three new actions are available in the project context menu (both the project list and the content pane ⋮ menu) when Google Drive is connected in plugin settings.

**For projects not yet linked to Drive:**

- **Link to Google Drive folder** — paste a Drive folder share link or ID to associate it with the project. Pick the vault folder whose contents map to that Drive folder. No files are transferred. Once linked, the existing Upload / Download actions become available.
- **Upload to Google Drive as new folder** — creates a brand-new Drive folder named after the project under a chosen parent (or My Drive root) and uploads the project's local folder into it in one step. The project is automatically linked to the new folder afterwards.

**For projects already linked to Drive:**

- **Unlink from Google Drive** — removes the Drive association from the project. Local files and Drive files are left completely untouched. A confirmation prompt is shown before unlinking.

The menu adapts automatically: link/upload-as-new are shown only when the project is unlinked; download/upload/unlink are shown only when a Drive folder is linked.

### CSS quality

Removed all `!important` declarations from `styles.css` — each override is now expressed through higher selector specificity instead, making the styles easier for themes to customise.
